criteria.reasoning: At 375px, content remained within the viewport with no horizontal overflow; chapters, pillars, form, session leads, and manager were reachable. Evidence: mobile-shared.png

criteria.name: breakpoint_choreography
criteria.description: Breakpoint behavior matches the spec: the mega-menu becomes a full-screen drawer at mobile widths, Get started renders as one column at mobile and three-up from the sm breakpoint, and the hero pull-in composition applies at 1024px and up
criteria.reasoning: Mobile showed a full-screen navigation drawer and one-column sections; desktop showed two-cell hero composition and three-up Get started cards. Evidence: drawer-shared.png

criteria.name: consistent_capitalization

criteria.reasoning: Headings, navigation, CTAs, and manager labels consistently use the same uppercase display convention. Evidence: manager-shared.png

criteria.name: institutional_copy_quality
criteria.description: Marketing copy reads as original, coherent institutional-tech voice for an enterprise blockchain product; error messages name the problem field and the fix, and empty states explain what belongs there and how to add it
criteria.reasoning: Copy was coherent enterprise blockchain/institutional-tech language; the manager empty state explained what belongs there and how to add it. Evidence: manager-empty-shared.png

criteria.name: placeholder_text_visible

criteria.reasoning: No Lorem ipsum, TODO text, template variables, or filler placeholders were visible. Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.reasoning: At 375px all four Why Ridge pillars were fully readable in document order as separate vertical cards without overlap. Evidence: mobile-shared.png

criteria.name: export_catalog_void_notch_panel
criteria.description: Export catalog renders as a dedicated panel with JSON and ICS preview regions plus Copy/Download/Load controls using the same void/notch visual language as the landing — not a plain unstyled browser dialog
criteria.reasoning: Export catalog appeared as a Ridge-styled dialog with JSON and ICS preview regions plus Copy, Export, and Load controls. Evidence: export-shared.png

criteria.description: Where the app renders marketing copy, it reads in a coherent institutional-tech voice for an enterprise blockchain product rather than casual template filler.
criteria.reasoning: Copy consistently presents Ridge as an enterprise/institutional blockchain infrastructure product in a coherent technical voice. Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: partner_marks_are_invented_names
criteria.description: Where the app renders trust-strip and partner marks, invented placeholder names appear — never real company logos, trademarks, or real-network proper nouns.
criteria.reasoning: Trust-strip marks use invented names such as Northstar Lab, Vector Union, Morrow Systems, and Civic Loom. Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: placeholder_copy_visible
criteria.description: Lorem ipsum or placeholder text (TODO, Lorem, asdf, or template variables like {{title}}) is visible anywhere in the shipped UI.
criteria.reasoning: No Lorem ipsum, TODO, asdf, or template-variable copy was observed. Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: prominent_copy_has_typos
criteria.description: Spelling or grammatical errors appear in prominent copy such as RIDGE GLOBAL EVENTS, Why Ridge pillar titles, or primary CTA labels.
criteria.reasoning: No spelling or grammatical errors were observed in prominent headings, pillar titles, or primary CTAs. Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: real_network_names_in_copy
criteria.description: Rendered copy names Avalanche, AVAX, avax.network, Snowtrace, or other real-network / real-company proper nouns instead of the Ridge placeholder brand.
criteria.reasoning: No Avalanche, AVAX, Snowtrace, or other real-network/company proper nouns were observed in rendered copy. Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: copy_truncated_or_overflowing
criteria.description: Truncated or overflowing text renders chapter headings, event titles, or form messages unreadable at 1440px or 375px.
criteria.reasoning: Desktop and compact layouts remained readable; headings and event titles wrapped without visible clipping. Evidence: mobile-overflow-shared.png

criteria.name: export_import_labels_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders export and import controls, labels use specific verbs (Export JSON, Export ICS, Copy, Download, Load catalog, Download leads JSON, Copy leads JSON, Undo last lead) rather than generic Submit/OK alone
criteria.reasoning: Visible controls use specific labels including Download leads JSON, Copy leads JSON, Undo last lead, and Export catalog. Evidence: success-and-leads-shared.png

criteria.name: copy_confirmation_states_outcome
criteria.description: Where the app renders Copy confirmation on Export or Session leads, the message states that the catalog or leads JSON was copied rather than a vague OK alone
criteria.reasoning: Copying leads visibly changes the control to Copied and shows “Copied the current ridge-session-leads.json text.” Evidence: copy-confirmation-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_session_leads_copy_explains
criteria.description: Where the app renders an empty Session leads state, the copy explains that contact submissions will appear there
criteria.reasoning: The empty state explicitly says valid contact submissions will appear there and be included in lead and catalog exports. Evidence: events-manager-shared.png

criteria.name: export_labels_generic_only
criteria.description: Export/import or Session leads controls use only generic Submit/OK labels with no Export JSON, Export ICS, Copy, Download, Load catalog, Download leads JSON, Copy leads JSON, or Undo last lead wording
criteria.reasoning: Generic-only Submit/OK labels are not used; export and session-lead controls have specific wording. Evidence: success-and-leads-shared.png

criteria.name: writing_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in rendered copy quality that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No significant browser-observable copy-quality defect outside the other criteria was observed. Evidence: initial-shared.png
